author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. Only has office hours for final. ATTEND LECTURE, tests have lecture content. Very test heavy, maximum 3 hw a week before each exam. Hw is always not the same as actual exam. Hope for a helpful TA who will teach you the content. Don't use textbook, lectures are not aligned with content. Go to all labs for credit and to write weekly reports. RUN! [Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. You'll do better if you've taken physics before. Only useful content in the syllabus is how the grades are broken up and a short description of what might be covered in the course. There is a textbook, but in my experience it isn't useful, lectures aren't aligned with textbook content. Lecture content is VERY IMPORTANT. Do not miss lectures or labs [Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY112. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. Rough professor, grades aren't in and theres one week of classes left, 70% exams 30% labs and exams are each 4 questions that he never even covered so they are almost impossible. He's setting students up for failure and he should not be a professor anymore. His lectures are slow and impossible to follow. Nothing posed on bright space. [Tags: Lecture heavy, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Very test heavy. He will give you points for working but the entire midterm is like 3 questions with 2-3 parts each, and the final is 6 questions some of them with no other parts. if you mess one up thats like 1/3rd or 1/6th of your grade for that test. Homework is tedious and not helpful for the exams. [Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. It's like watching an XQC stream if he tries to explain it to you. He'll focus on one thing and overdo it making other topics moving on difficult to understand. [Tags: Tough grader, Get ready to read, Lecture heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 2.0/5, Grade received: B. This guy teaches slow, talks slow, moves slow, and does everything slow except for leaving the lecture hall. I learned nothing in this class, all the material is included in AP Physics 2. If you need to reach him, good luck. After a month of trying to reach him I had to email the department head instead. No office hours either.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: B. Be prepared to learn physics by yourself. Lectures are not helpful at all. The homework given is either extremely easier or downright impossible. Grading is fairly lenient and even gives some recitation 100s before tests. Curve is nice though. [Tags: Tough grader, Lots of homework, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: C. Horrific professor. If you're like me, someone who doesn't learn or understand topics at ALL from textbooks, you'll be in for a fun time. His tests are not even close in difficulty to the practice exams he provides, and he genuinely expects you to be a genius physics student to be able to actually do well on exams. Do not take him. [Tags: Tough grader, Test heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Awful experience with this course. The course is all over the place there is no consistency with HW, he would post the HW at random times between Friday morning and Saturday night and the exams are a guessing game about what's going to be on it as he provides review that ends up being nothing relating to the exams. This course needs a new professor [Tags: Tough grader, Get ready to read, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. He can't lecture at all. When we covered Archimedes' principle he talked more about the story of how archimedes figured it out instead of the actual physics. Lecture attendance isn't mandatory, but recitation is. In the recitation, the homeworks are graded very tough, but the quizzes are brain-dead easy. Just read the textbook and skip lecture. [Tags: Tough grader, Get ready to read, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: B. He definitely knows his stuff , however his way of teaching the material is all over the place. Whether it be a white/chalkboard it gets a bit a messy to the point that you will need to rely on the textbook more which I cannot stress enough. As far as recitation, attendance is mandatory, and quiz questions are made on the spot from him. [Tags: Get ready to read, Lecture heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: C. Awful experience. [Tags: Tough grader, Lecture heavy, Test heavy]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. My pet rat could teach better physics

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. This professor does not have a clear lesson plans. You are going to study on your own. He does not have office hours.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Don't take this class if you're new to physics, TA literally told us this class is review for students who took it in HS after our first midterm, so if you didn't you'll have a hard time in this class (like me). You only get graded on your midterms/final/weekly hw. Lectures don't help and he doesn't do practice problems in class. [Tags: Lecture heavy, Test heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. DO NOT GO TO LECTURES, but do attend the quizzes. The quizzes are based off of the homework. The class average is 30% on the midterms. You will NOT learn anything from him. If you want your GPA to diminish and if you want a professor who DOES NOT CARE about you or his job, take him. Given study guides are irrelevant and not on the midterms/exams. [Tags: Tough grader, Get ready to read]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. Lectures are extremely boring. Every class just consists of the prof writing on the board and talking with his back turned to students. Barely takes questions from people. He also likes to post HW last minute so you're only gonna have 3-4 days to finish the HW and hand it in. [Tags: Get ready to read, Lots of homework, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. Horrible dont take [Tags: Get ready to read, Test heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5, Grade received: B. Lectures were really boring. He's a textbook prof so he reads from textbook verbatim. Could skip lectures and just read textbook. Midterm and finals are not that difficult if you are somewhat good at math and physics.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 2.0/5, Grade received: A. Lectures focus on long derivations but gave me more insight; not much application though. The recitation quizzes are based on HW problems, so if you know what you're doing they aren't difficult. The exams aren't much harder than the quizzes/HW but they are short. Grading is very lenient. Overall, if you're confident in physics the class isn't hard. [Tags: Test heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text:
Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: B. Class goes like this:

learn about topic
explanation of topic
derivation of formula/equations
repeat for 80 minutes

test: 3 questions, all applications :(

yeah you can pass his class just skipping class and reading textbook(many of classmates did this)
Curve is awesome though, 80+ is an A [Tags: Get ready to read]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. Graf literally does not introduce himself, and immediately goes into lecture mode. He's never at his office, NO OFFICE HOURS! One can only haggle him either immediately before or after class. So glad I will never see this man again! Very awkward and incredibly inaccessible. Get this man in Congress. [Tags: Lecture heavy, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY131. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: F. Boycott this specimen fr

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 3.0/5, Difficulty 1.0/5, Grade received: A. I only went to class for the midterms and final, so I can't comment on his teaching ability. author: anonymous student

If you want to get in touch with him talk to him in person after the lecture he is hard to reach online and isn't always at his office hours. AVOID HIM. author: anonymous student

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5, Grade received: B. BY FAR the worst professor I've ever had. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5, Grade received: B. Honestly, his lectures were pretty good. They were always interesting and well-paced. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. Don't take ANY classes with him; he doesn't care about students. Didn't have TAs or put any grades online. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: B. The professor only derives formulas and briefly goes over the history of how they were derived. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 3.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: A. Graf isnt as bad as others say. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY131. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. If you're here to do engineering or physics, you should have no problem in this class. If you are not, but are required to take a natural science elective, and have little to no background in physics - you will not make it out of this class unscathed. [Tags: Tough grader]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. [Tags: Get ready to read, Graded by few things]

author: anonymous student

author: anonymous student

text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY131. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. It's great that he derives formulas, but spending too much time on that is not sufficient enough for students to learn actual physics.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY131. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: A. Erlend Graf is a terrible professor. author: anonymous student

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY126. Quality 5.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Professor Graf has a great teaching method and watching him derive formulas has made me better at doing it as well. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Graf is a nice guy at heart, but he doesn't really teach the material. You'll probably learn more in the lab than in the lectures themselves, but that's entirely dependent on who your TA is. Spends a lot of time in class deriving equations, but never actually puts them into use. Unless you've taken physics before, you probably will not learn much. [Tags: LOTS OF HOMEWORK, LECTURE HEAVY]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 5.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: A. Really very fair. The people here that gave bad reviews obviously did not do a bit of homework [Tags: Tough Grader, Gives good feedback, Participation matters]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. You need to go to class and take notes because he free-hands every lecture. Goes over theory and gives formulas but doesnt really tell you how to apply them. HW is actually really difficult. author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 3.0/5. With my limited experiences in the lecture, Graf is an old professor who is past his prime for teaching. He clearly knows his stuff but having him as a physics professor for an 8 AM physics lecture is a huge mistake. His monotone voice makes it hard to stay awake in class but his handwriting is very neat. The physics recitation is more helpful [Tags: LECTURE HEAVY]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. By far the worst professor I've ever had. He spends the entire class going over the simple derivation of the formula and only does a super simple example. The homeworks are on a completely different level and he is absolutely useless. The greatest part is his practice tests are a joke compared to the real tests. Avoid him no matter what you do. [Tags: Tough Grader, Skip class? You won't pass., Tests are tough]

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 3.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Intelligent, but sometimes difficult to understand. Yes, he does go off on a tangent and sometimes it helps, sometimes it doesn't. Tough quizzes (if you're in his recitations) & tests, but physics is already hard and confusing in itself. With or without him, you must work hard individually. He'll help you if you ask him after class or office hours.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. doesnt matter if you show up to class because you will stil get a 50 on the test. doesnt teach u anything you can use, lab write ups suck if u can avoid thiss class do so and take it somewhere else over the summer

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY127. Quality 5.0/5, Difficulty 1.0/5. Tough but very fair. Really smart professor but doesn't act like he's a know-it-all

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 4.0/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. As unfortunate as it might sound, you actually need to pay attention to understand physics. That being said, yes he does do a great deal of formula derivation in lecture, but if you can work with raw formulas, then you can also work with numbers and formulas. The derivations from lecture are actually much more useful than others make them appear.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.0/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. Goes off into a tangent, trying to figure out the concepts for himself rather than explain them properly to the class. Very disorganized and not structured. Does not teach at all how to apply the concepts and formulas he derives for himself on the board. No wonder the class average is a 40. His out of the box questions don't help learn the material

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 1.5/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. Terrible at teaching, he only teaches theory and doesn't teach you how to apply the theory to problems. He never did practice problems in class.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 2.5/5, Difficulty 4.0/5, Grade received: A. He mostly derives equations that he doesn't teach you how to apply in problems you encounter. The course is poorly organized- you never know how you are doing. You do get a cheat sheet, but I still got a 45 on the first exam. I got an A- in the course, so yes the curve helps. My advice: make sure you have EVERY equation taught on that cheat sheet.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 2.5/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. He is pretty clear on some parts, and uses 1 class day to review for the exam. He does not teach you how to apply the formulas though.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY119. Quality 3.5/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. I actually really liked Professor Graf. Yes, this course can be challenging for non-science majors.. but, I found that Graf explained the material very well and clearly. My only issue is that he didn't go over enough practice problems in class (only covered theory) - which made HW and tests hard. Bright side? 8x11 Cheat Cheats & Big Curves!

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 4.5/5, Difficulty 2.0/5. I actually really liked him. I took and failed 131 (D) w/ Deshpande but I got an A in 125 w/ Graf. 8x11 front and back cheat sheets were awesome. He's a monotone lecturer but he is clear in his teachings. I went to nearly all 8:30 lectures b/c I learned so well w/ him. I'm excited to take 126 w/ him over the summer.

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 2.0/5, Difficulty 5.0/5. he has got to be the worst professor ever! he stands in the front of the room and works out problems for himself. He tends to make things way more confusing than they really are. AVOID HIM

author: anonymous student
text: Review of Erlend Graf (Physics), PHY125. Quality 3.5/5, Difficulty 4.0/5. The class is pretty difficult and I suggest you only take it if it is required of your major, otherwise Graf is a decent professor. The class is pretty early and his tone can be quite the lullaby, but as long as you study for several hours you might be able to get a decent grade. He allows cheat sheets, and FYI his practice exams are rubbish.
